title: uragoga argentata
intro_study: According to "Biomolecules", Ursonic acid (UNA), which is extracted from Urugoga argentata, exerts potential benefits including inhibiting the survival and proliferation of various types of cancer cells, as well as exhibiting antihyperglycemic, anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant activities.
side_effects: Uragoga argentata can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and headaches due to its high levels of toxic compounds. Ingesting large amounts of the plant can also cause vomiting, diarrhea, and stomach ulcers. Prolonged use or misuse of uragoga argentata can lead to kidney damage, liver problems, and even seizures in extreme cases. It's essential to consult a medical professional before using any plant-based remedy to avoid these potential side effects.
